name: dependency-security
description: Enforce dependency security scanning and SBOM generation. Use when adding dependencies, reviewing package.json, or during security audits. Covers OWASP dependency check, npm audit, and supply chain security.
capabilities: Run npm audit to detect critical and high severity vulnerabilities in project dependencies
Generate SBOM documents using CycloneDX or SPDX formats for compliance and transparency
Configure .npmrc security settings to prevent malicious script execution during installation
Integrate security checks into CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions or similar platforms
Check for deprecated packages and outdated dependencies that may contain security flaws
Verify package integrity using lockfiles and npm ci for reproducible builds
author: Doyajin174
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing no executable code. All flagged patterns are false positives from code examples and documentation text. The skill provides legitimate security guidance for npm dependency scanning, SBOM generation, and supply chain security best practices.
valueStatement: Dependency vulnerabilities are a leading cause of security breaches in modern applications. This skill provides clear guidance for scanning npm packages, generating Software Bill of Materials documents, and implementing supply chain security practices.
userTitle: Scan dependencies for vulnerabilities and generate SBOM
limitations: Does not automatically fix vulnerabilities - requires manual review and intervention for each issue
Limited to Node.js and npm ecosystem - does not cover Python, Ruby, Java or other package managers
Does not scan system-level dependencies or native binary packages outside npm registry
Security scanner effectiveness depends on npm registry vulnerability database completeness

useCases.title: Automate security audits in CI/CD
useCases.description: Integrate dependency scanning into automated pipelines to catch vulnerabilities before production deployment

useCases.title: Review dependencies before adding
useCases.description: Verify new packages are safe, well-maintained, and license-compatible before including them in projects

useCases.title: Generate SBOM for compliance
useCases.description: Create and maintain Software Bill of Materials documents for regulatory compliance and audit requirements

promptTemplates.title: Quick security scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Run npm audit on the current project and report any critical or high severity vulnerabilities with recommended fixes

promptTemplates.title: Generate SBOM document
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a CycloneDX SBOM for this project and save it to sbom.json with all dependency information

promptTemplates.title: Safe dependency updates
promptTemplates.prompt: Check for outdated packages and create a safe update plan that prioritizes security patches without breaking changes

promptTemplates.title: CI security integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs npm audit and blocks merges if any high or critical vulnerabilities are detected

bestPractices: Always commit package-lock.json to ensure reproducible builds and prevent supply chain attacks
Set ignore-scripts=true in .npmrc to prevent malicious pre-install and post-install script execution
Generate SBOM documents regularly and store them as release artifacts for compliance and transparency
antiPatterns: Using npm install instead of npm ci in CI pipelines - may install different versions than lockfile specifies
Ignoring moderate severity vulnerabilities without documenting decision and tracking for future resolution
Adding packages without checking maintenance status, download counts, or reviewing recent security advisories

faq.question: Does this skill work with yarn or pnpm package managers?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on npm commands. For yarn, use yarn audit. For pnpm, use pnpm audit with similar syntax.

faq.question: What severity level should block production builds?
faq.answer: Block on critical and high severity issues. Track moderate issues for resolution. Low severity can be deferred to regular updates.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with GitHub Actions or GitLab CI?
faq.answer: Yes. Add a step that runs npm audit --audit-level=high and exits with failure code if vulnerabilities are found.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to external services?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance only. Commands like npm audit make read-only API calls to the npm registry vulnerability database.

faq.question: What if npm audit returns no results or errors?
faq.answer: This can indicate an outdated npm version or network issues. Update npm to the latest version and verify registry connectivity.

faq.question: How does this compare to Snyk, WhiteSource, or commercial scanners?
faq.answer: npm audit uses the npm registry database for free. Commercial tools have larger databases, better reporting, and advanced features but require setup.
